pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 26 defines PROJECT_DIR matching metadata
setup_task.sh line 43 creates BLOAT_FILE=$ASSETS_DIR/onboarding_deprecated.mp4 where ASSETS_DIR=$PROJECT_DIR/app/src/main/assets; export_result.sh line 8 uses same path
Verified: 5 * 1024 * 1024 = 5242880 bytes. Matches verifier.py line 43: TARGET_SIZE_BYTES = 5 * 1024 * 1024. Also stated as 'under 5MB' in task description
setup_task.sh line 48 creates bloat file with: dd if=/dev/urandom of=$BLOAT_FILE bs=1M count=30, which creates exactly 30MB

privileged_info_summary: The task reduces APK size from over 25MB to under 5MB (5242880 bytes exactly) by removing a synthetic 30MB bloat file (onboarding_deprecated.mp4) located at app/src/main/assets/. The project (NewsReaderApp) is created synthetically from a CalculatorApp template during task setup. The bloat file is created with /dev/urandom data to simulate uncompressible asset bloat. All metadata values are accurate and consistent across setup_task.sh, verifier.py, and export_result.sh.
